programme.title: Bamay
programme.desc: Slow TV is back, celebrating stunning landscapes of Countries across Australia. Sit back and relax with the healing powers of Country.

programme.title: Bamay
programme.desc: Slow TV is back, celebrating stunning landscapes of Countries across Australia. Sit back and relax with the healing powers of Country.

programme.title: Toi Time
programme.desc: Jojo builds a cardboard city and Fluffy the cat has to be rescued by the Cardboard City heroes, Officer Maia, Super Jojo and Firefighter Tama.

programme.title: Waabiny Time
programme.desc: My Moort, my family make me djoorabiny, they make me happy.

programme.title: Bird's Eye View
programme.desc: The birds decide to get away from the noise of the city. Unfortunately, the Humans have the same idea. What are they doing now?

programme.title: Aussie Bush Tales
programme.desc: Moort's family is surprised when a huge bird crashes into the snowy mountain near where they are sheltering.

programme.title: The Mysterious Cities of Gold
programme.desc: Our young heroes and Ambrosius have managed to enter the second City of Gold. Esteban and Zia receive a message delivered by holograms of The Sages of Mu and Atlantis.

programme.title: Bushwhacked!
programme.desc: Kayne and Kamil set off to Uluru in search of Australia's greatest monitor, the perentie, but not without meeting some very special desert folk along the way!

programme.title: Motown Magic
programme.sub-title: War And Tracks Of My Tears
programme.desc: Imaginative boy Ben transforms his city by bringing colourful street art to life, armed with a magic paintbrush and the classic sounds of motown.

programme.title: The Magic Canoe
programme.desc: When Julie gets stuck in the pond, she is too embarrassed and proud to ask for help. On an expedition, she will understand that everyone needs help sometimes and that it's okay to ask for it.

programme.title: Spartakus and the Sun Beneath the Sea
programme.desc: Under the plastic palm trees of their inflatable island, it's holiday time for the hackers. Our heroes want to reopen an old passage that has become impassable.

programme.title: Bamay
programme.sub-title: North Stradbroke Island, Quandamooka Country Interstitial
programme.desc: Slow TV is back with more beautiful Bamay. Bamay III celebrates great Australian islands and saltwater country. Sit back and relax with the healing powers of Country.

programme.title: Bamay
programme.desc: Slow TV is back with more beautiful Bamay. Bamay III celebrates great Australian islands and saltwater country. Sit back and relax with the healing powers of Country.

programme.title: Going Places With Ernie Dingo
programme.desc: Ernie travels to the Great Barrier Reef to meet people who call this unique part of the world home. He catches up with Yindinji elder Gudju Gudju, Jamie a toxicologist, and Gareth skipper of Monsoon.

programme.title: Generations Of Men
programme.desc: A haunting revisionist western inspired by poet Judith Wright's family history, and the first narrative work to feature Barada and Darumbal languages. Set in May Downs, 1859, it follows a white family's journey halted by childbirth. Directed by Joanna Joy with Barada and Darumbal collaboration.

programme.title: NITV News Update
programme.desc: NITV News recaptures the highlights of Indigenous news around the country.

programme.title: Wild, Smart and Deadly
programme.desc: Avoiding predators and competition for space, food, and mates is a priority for every animal. But before you can attack, you need to be able to defend yourself.

programme.title: Free The People
programme.desc: After the Earl's get kicked off of Black Lives Matter Plaza, many other organizations ramp up their efforts. A few people splinter away to form Good Trouble Co-Op.

programme.title: 3 1/2 Minutes
programme.desc: Dissects the aftermath of the murder of 17-year-old Jordan Davis and the trial of Michael Dunn who, in 2012, shot him repeatedly at a Florida gas station.

programme.title: Dogfight
programme.desc: River Phoenix and Lili Taylor star in a bittersweet story about losing innocence and discovering love.

programme.title: Yiyili
programme.desc: A Gooniyandi man speaks about the importance of teaching culture to the kids in his local community and his mission to keep language alive.

programme.title: Lagau Danalaig: An Island Life
programme.desc: With an idyllic island lifestyle as the backdrop, we find out what makes Badu unique through the stories of the people as expressed in their art and culture.

programme.title: Going Places With Ernie Dingo
programme.desc: Ernie's trip to tropical north Queensland finds him meeting a respected Kuku Yalanji Elder, a river guide who's made a sea change, and an environmentalist who is determined to change attitudes.

programme.title: NITV On The Road
programme.desc: From our travelling music series, this program showcases veterans and newcomers alike as they perform at the Barunga Festival 2016.

programme.title: NITV On The Road
programme.desc: From our travelling music series, this program showcases veterans and newcomers alike as they perform at the Barunga Festival 2016.

programme.title: NITV on the Road: Boomerang Festival
programme.desc: Boomerang is a festival held in Byron Bay over the long weekend. It is run by Rhoda Roberts, the creator of the Dreaming Festival, and is a mixture of Australian and international Indigenous acts.

programme.title: Bamay
programme.desc: Slow TV is back, celebrating stunning landscapes of Countries across Australia. Sit back and relax with the healing powers of Country.

programme.title: Bamay
programme.desc: Slow TV is back, celebrating stunning landscapes of Countries across Australia. Sit back and relax with the healing powers of Country.

programme.title: Toi Time
programme.desc: The wheels on the bus that Tama builds go round and round and Buzz shares his toy boats with Tama in Takaro Time.

programme.title: Waabiny Time
programme.desc: Moorditj walang, good health is about looking after our bodies every day. It's solid koolangka!

programme.title: Bird's Eye View
programme.desc: Big Jim's mirror has gone missing! As much as he hates admitting it, he's missing his sassy cellmate. A bird hunt ensues, with Tui as a self-appointed Sherlock Holmes.

programme.title: Aussie Bush Tales
programme.desc: It's been raining for so long that everything and everyone is thoroughly wet and cranky.

programme.title: The Mysterious Cities of Gold
programme.desc: Our heroes have escaped The City as it was closing in upon itself. So has Ambrosius, who has stolen a matrix of olishark from the City of Gold.

programme.title: Bushwhacked!
programme.desc: Kamil challenges Kayne's inner cowboy to conquer a rodeo bull ride and become a protection athlete, aka rodeo clown, at a professional rodeo.

programme.title: Motown Magic
programme.sub-title: I'm A Roadrunner And Smiling Faces Sometimes
programme.desc: Ben and Ella are forced to become roommates when Uncle Rod moves in. But it's not long before they get on each other's nerves and Ben declares war! When Ben can't seem to win, he visits Street Art World where a conflict between Jimmy Mack and Dancing Machine helps Ben understand the futility of war.

programme.title: The Magic Canoe
programme.desc: While she's playing with two little porcupines, Pam stands on the tail of one of them. Claiming it was an accident, she refuses to apologise. Later, she realises that apologising is a nice thing to do.

programme.title: Spartakus and the Sun Beneath the Sea
programme.desc: Fleeing from the pirate scooters, Tehrig steps inside a huge crystal formation which turns out to be inhabited.

programme.title: The World According to Grandpa
programme.desc: Stanley asks \.

programme.title: Chums
programme.desc: A rag tag group of furry friends discover their relationship to the animals, the environment, and Mother Earth.

programme.title: Wild, Smart and Deadly
programme.desc: Avoiding predators and competition for space, food, and mates is a priority for every animal. But before you can attack, you need to be able to defend yourself.

programme.title: NITV News Update
programme.desc: NITV News recaptures the highlights of Indigenous news around the country.

programme.title: Pioneers of Love
programme.desc: The second part of Pioneers of Love looks at how after the The Bolshevik Revolution of 1918 and the backlash from the Brisbane Red Flag riots, many of the Illin family left for South America. But the law of the early 20th century prevented full-blood Aborigines from travelling. Thus Leandro remained behind with Kitty and their children. Disillusioned, Leandro withdrew to a remote outback location, taking his family with him. Tragedy struck when Kitty, isolated and far from medical assistance, died after enduring a complicated birth. With his wife and newborn child dead, Leandro moved the family closer to civilisation, at Greenvale station. (Commissioned by SBS, in English) (Documentary Series) (Rpt) G CC WS.

programme.title: Night Parrot Stories
programme.desc: Somewhere in the remote deserts of Australia lived an elusive nocturnal bird called the Night Parrot. Virtually nothing is known of its ecology.

programme.title: Road Open
programme.desc: Djarindjin is a medium-sized Aboriginal community located 170km from Broome in the Kimberley Region of Western Australia, within the shire of Broome.

programme.title: Road Open
programme.desc: Stories from the community in Beagle Bay.

programme.title: Island Echoes with Nornie Bero
programme.sub-title: The Sunrise and Flavours of Horn Island
programme.desc: This episode is a heartfelt tribute to Horn Island's charm, its people, and the deep connection between the community and the sea.

programme.title: The Cook Up with Adam Liaw
programme.desc: If you want dinner in 20 minutes, Adam, owner of Bella Bruta Luke Powell, and owner of Jeow Thi Li have got you covered.

programme.title: Spartakus and the Sun Beneath the Sea
programme.desc: Our heroes return to the frozen layer of Icelandis, intent on unlocking the secret of the ghost ship. Embarking alone, Spartakus finally goes to meet the mysterious captain.

programme.title: The Mysterious Cities of Gold
programme.desc: Our heroes are surrounded by a troop of Bedouins. Their intrigued chief observes the medallion and then returns it to Zia, apologising for the young thief: a Bedouin never flies.

programme.title: The Mysterious Cities of Gold
programme.desc: Aboard the Condor, Esteban is worried about Mendoza, but his friends remind him that their priority is to stop Ambrosius from appropriating Mu wisdom to make weapons.

programme.title: Little J & Big Cuz
programme.desc: Little J longs to join 'budda' George on country, while Ally looks up to tidda Big Cuz.

programme.title: Motown Magic
programme.sub-title: Runaway Child, Running Child And Don't Mess With Bill
programme.desc: Imaginative boy Ben transforms his city by bringing colourful street art to life, armed with a magic paintbrush and the classic sounds of motown.

programme.title: Harlem Globetrotters: Play It Forward
programme.desc: The Harlem Globetrotters perform for servicemen and their families during a visit to Moody Air Force Base in Georgia. Thunder pays a visit to his hometown of Chesapeake.

programme.title: Garma: Celebrating 25 Years
programme.desc: Stories from Yolngu Country celebrating 25 years of Garma.

programme.title: The 77 Percent
programme.desc: Africa is home to a large number of youth as they constitute 77 percent of the continent's population. A few ambitious youngsters come together to share their vision for the continent's future.

programme.title: Bamay
programme.desc: Slow TV is back, celebrating stunning landscapes of Countries across Australia. Sit back and relax with the healing powers of Country.

programme.title: NITV News Update
programme.desc: NITV News recaptures the highlights of Indigenous news around the country.

programme.title: The Big Dry
programme.desc: It's October in Zambia's Luangwa Valley. The hottest and harshest time of year. But this year dry season conditions are exacerbated by a global El Nino event... the Valley is in the throes of drought.

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are? Aus
programme.desc: Tom Gleeson unravels the shadowy past of his four times great grandfather Thomas Carpenter, transported to Australia after narrowly escaping the gallows. He also investigates ancestor John Mitton, whose connection to infamous goldfield riots conceals a truth far more complex than family legends suggested.

programme.title: Living Black
programme.desc: Living Black returns in 2026 with another series of in depth conversations and investigations. Join Walkley Award winner Karla Grant as she explores the issues facing Indigenous Australians.

programme.title: The Skin of Others
programme.desc: This a story of modern Australia - its violent past and its future potential - told through the extraordinary life of Aboriginal World War I soldier Douglas Grant (1885-1951). Featuring acclaimed Indigenous actor Balang Tom E Lewis in his final performance (as Douglas Grant) and guest appearances from Max Cullen and Archie Roach, this film movingly interweaves the lives of Grant and Lewis: two truly remarkable men.

programme.title: Redacted
programme.desc: A fictional documentary discusses the effects the Iraq war has had on soldiers and local people through interviews with members of an American military unit, the media and local Iraqis.

programme.title: Trans and Pregnant
programme.desc: A thought provoking and inspiring documentary about two men who are on a journey to parenthood. Not by adoption or surrogacy, but by Frankie, a trans man, carrying the baby.

programme.title: Going Places With Ernie Dingo
programme.desc: Ernie visits the Great Ocean Road and catches up with a local Gunditjmara/Kirrae Whurrong songman. He meets a passionate foodologist, and spends time with an entrepreneur with a taste for adventure.

programme.title: NITV on the Road: Boomerang Festival
programme.desc: Boomerang is a festival held in Byron Bay over the long weekend. It is run by Rhoda Roberts, the creator of the Dreaming Festival, and is a mixture of Australian and international Indigenous acts.

programme.title: NITV on the Road: Boomerang Festival
programme.desc: Boomerang is a festival held in Byron Bay over the long weekend. It is run by Rhoda Roberts, the creator of the Dreaming Festival, and is a mixture of Australian and international Indigenous acts.

programme.title: NITV on the Road: Boomerang Festival
programme.desc: Boomerang is a festival held in Byron Bay over the long weekend. It is run by Rhoda Roberts, the creator of the Dreaming Festival, and is a mixture of Australian and international Indigenous acts.
